sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

print("=== Small objects ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] < 100:
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} px={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']} children={n['children']}")

print("\n=== Rows 30-32 ===")
for i in range(30, 33):
    print(f"{i}: {ascii_lines[i][8:56]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Now I see:
- N(W) moved from cols 18-20 to cols 15-17 (left by 3)
- The container at cols 11-15 has changed shape slightly (boundary changed from [[32, 11], [32, 15], [36, 15], [36, 11]] to [[32, 11], [32, 14], [33, 15], [36, 15], [36, 11]])
- The container's pixel count changed from 16 to 15
Looking at row 32: `gwwccccNNNwwwwwwwwwNNNwwwppppppwwwwwwwwwwwwwwwgg`
The container (c) is now at cols 11-14 (4 wide instead of 5), and the N token is at cols 15-17.
So the N(W) token is now adjacent to the container. The container seems to have "opened" or changed shape to accommodate the token.
Let me try LEFT again to see if N(W) enters the container.

# N(W) is now at cols 15-17, adjacent to the container at cols 11-14
# Let me try LEFT again to move it into the container
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
